definitions: A concluding passage of a piece or movement, typically forming an addition to the basic structure.
The concluding part of a story, event, or series of events.
The part of a syllable after the nucleus (usually a vowel), typically a consonant or consonant cluster.
examples: The symphony ended with a powerful coda.
His resignation was a sad coda to a brilliant career.
The word 'cat' has a 't' in the coda.
